denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

This letter advises you of our determination regarding your provider's request on 07/13/2026 for Lanadelumab prophylaxis related to Hereditary angioedema (D84.1). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. A less costly modality is appropriate before Lanadelumab prophylaxis for Hereditary angioedema (D84.1). Plan policy requires step-down diagnostic or therapeutic approach.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with modality substitution. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.
clinical_context: The 27-year-old member has Hereditary angioedema (D84.1). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Lanadelumab prophylaxis recommended as the next appropriate step. Monthly attacks; C4 chronically low; failed androgens due to side effects. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that modality substitution bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
